critical_page_1_facts: provide 1 1/4 inch conduit from each telecom outlet to nearest accessible ceiling or cable tray
provide double-gang back box with single-gang reducer bracket for each telecom outlet
for each WAP provide 2-port surface mounted biscuit box
all empty conduits must include minimum two nylon drag cords and bushed ends
all cables labeled at both ends 6 inches from termination
provide 12 inches slack for each telecom cable at workstation end
provide 20 feet slack for wireless access points
terminate WAP cables on dedicated patch panel
all voice and data horizontal cables plenum rated
for wireless access points TC mounts WAP bracket and WAP, both owner provided
field coordinate exact WAP locations with URI prior to installation
CCTV cables terminate on patch panel dedicated to WAPs and CCTV
jack colors: red wireless, green cameras, black wall phones/elevators, blue LAN
all data cables blue unless otherwise noted
critical_floorplan_facts: run all tel/data devices on lower level to 0TC1
run all tel/data devices on many floors to nearest tele data closet
some first-floor devices run to 1TC2 based on column-line note
clean room tel/data locations require stainless steel faceplate
exterior WAP locations coordinated with architect and GC
if exterior WAPs cannot mount to structure, place within planters
EC provides 3/4 inch conduit to planters
exterior WAPs mounted in weatherproof enclosure
critical_riser_facts: 25-pair CAT5e copper cable from MDF to each IDF
12-strand singlemode armored fiber optic cable from MDF to each IDF
48-strand OSP singlemode armored fiber between new engineering building and Kirk building
terminate all fiber with LC connectors and loaded fiber panels at both ends
50-pair CAT3 OSP copper terminated on 110 blocks
100-pair building entrance protection blocks at both ends
voice and data riser cables run in separate conduit sleeves
provide fireproofing for conduits and sleeves
pull box required for conduit section exceeding 180 degrees bend or 100 feet
each conduit or sleeve has bushed ends and minimum one pull string
all tel/data rooms tied back to MDF with #6 AWG ground or main electrical ground
critical_detail_facts: bond each equipment rack to grounding busbar with #4 AWG insulated ground wire
bond all ladder rack and cable tray joints with #4 AWG insulated ground wire
do not bond ladder rack or cable tray to equipment racks
graph_expectations: idf and mdf participate in riser topology
rack and ladder rack grounded via bonding details
